benchmark_details.name: MedHallTune
benchmark_details.overview: MedHallTune is a large-scale benchmark designed specifically to evaluate and mitigate hallucinations in medical vision-language models (VLMs). It comprises over 100,000 images and 1,000,000 instruction pairs, including both hallucination and non-hallucination samples with ground-truth annotations.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To address hallucinations of vision-language models within the medical domain and enhance their reliability for clinical applications.

data.source: Over 100,000 annotated images sampled from well-annotated datasets sourced from the PubMed database.
data.annotation: Ground-truth annotations for hallucination and non-hallucination samples.

methodology.calculation: Evaluation metrics rated on a scale from 1 to 10, assessing correctness, relevance, detail, and patient safety risk.
methodology.interpretation: Higher scores indicate better performance across all evaluation metrics.
methodology.validation: Comprehensive evaluation of state-of-the-art VLMs including both general and medical applications.
